pubmed_links: [Bassiony et al. (2025) GRIK1 variants and autism spectrum disorders in Egyptian children](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41327126/); [Chatterjee et al. (2022) GRIK1 risk variants and deficiency in Indian ADHD probands](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36323684/); [Hu et al. (2022) Ultrarare loss-of-function mutations in kainate receptor genes associated with schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35629206/)
summary: GRIK1 encodes a kainate-type ionotropic glutamate receptor subunit and has been associated with multiple neurodevelopmental and neuropsychiatric conditions, with risk variants and loss-of-function mutations implicated in autism spectrum disorder, ADHD, and schizophrenia. Evidence from case-control and family-based studies supports a role for disrupted kainate receptor signaling in these disorders, though effect sizes remain modest. (emerging evidence)
